higher, each and every loss. the higher applicable amount has been deducted from the adjusted loss. keywords: adjustment, excess, deductible, percentage, minimum adjustment excess / deductible theft specific the policy / certificate is subject to the applicable theft deductible. having deducted the deductible from the adjusted loss, the net recommended amount is rm[amount], subject to all other policy terms and conditions. keywords: adjustment, excess, deductible, theft adjustment salvage stock retains value the affected stock / items retain salvage value despite the damage sustained. based on enquiries / offers received / salvage assessment, a salvage value of rm[amount] has been taken into account and deducted from the adjusted loss. keywords: adjustment, salvage, stock, salvage value, deducted adjustment salvage no economical salvage we considered the possibility of salvage recovery. however, based on the nature and condition of the affected items, including contamination / severe soaking / low residual value, the items do not appear to retain economical salvage value. keywords: adjustment, salvage, no salvage value, uneconomical adjustment salvage salvage tender / offer a salvage exercise / enquiry was carried out and offers were invited from salvage merchants. based on the highest offer / reasonable salvage value obtained, the amount of rm[amount] has been adopted in our adjustment. keywords: adjustment, salvage tender, salvage offer, merchants adjustment average sum insured adequate based on the information available and our estimated value at risk, the sum insured / sum covered appears adequate. accordingly, the application of average is not warranted. keywords: adjustment, average, adequacy, sum insured adequate adjustment average underinsurance applies based on our estimated value at risk, the sum insured / sum covered appears inadequate. the condition of average is therefore applicable and the loss has been adjusted proportionately in accordance with the policy wording. keywords: adjustment, average, underinsurance, value at risk adjustment average first loss no average the relevant section is arranged on a first loss / without average basis. accordingly, there is no necessity to apply the condition of average, subject to the applicable limit and policy terms. keywords: adjustment, average, first loss, without average adjustment betterment replacement improves condition a deduction for betterment has been considered as the proposed replacement / repair would place the insured / participant in a better position than immediately before the loss. the deduction reflects the improvement, upgraded specification or replacement of aged components with new items. keywords: adjustment, betterment, improvement, new replacement adjustment betterment scope includes upgrading certain claimed works appear to include upgrading / improvement beyond reinstatement of the insured damage. the cost relating to such improvement has been adjusted / disallowed to ensure the claim is limited to reasonable reinstatement of the damage caused by the insured event. keywords: adjustment, betterment, upgrade, improvement, disallowed adjustment betterment no betterment deduction no deduction for betterment is applied as the repair works are considered necessary to reinstate the damaged property to a condition substantially similar to its pre loss condition and do not materially improve the property beyond its original specification. keywords: adjustment, betterment, no deduction, reinstatement

documents.title: Suggested Wording — RVC Invoice Error But Insurer Instructed Payment
documents.excerpt: Reinstatement Value Clause allows settlement on repair / replacement / reinstatement basis.

headings.text: Key Point
Application Sample
Facts
Suggested wording
Suggested Wording — RVC Invoice Error But Insurer Instructed Payment
Short wording
More formal wording
Protective wording
If the invoice amount is wrong but RVC still payable
If insurer directly instructs payment
RVC Compliance — Invoice Submitted / Error Noted / Insurer Advised Payment
Circumstances
Our Review
Insurer’s Instruction
Suggested Wording
Key Point

documents.searchText: suggested wording — rvc invoice error but insurer instructed payment reinstatement value clause rvc.md policy & clauses key point application sample facts suggested wording suggested wording — rvc invoice error but insurer instructed payment short wording more formal wording protective wording if the invoice amount is wrong but rvc still payable if insurer directly instructs payment rvc compliance — invoice submitted / error noted / insurer advised payment circumstances our review insurer’s instruction suggested wording key point endorsement clauses simple meaning reinstatement value clause allows settlement on repair / replacement / reinstatement basis. usually “new for old”. depreciation may not be deducted if the reinstatement condition is complied with. insured usually needs to actually reinstate and provide supporting invoices. key point full rvc benefit normally depends on actual reinstatement. application sample facts building / machinery damaged. policy is subject to rvc. insured submitted invoices for reinstatement. suggested wording the policy is noted to be subject to reinstatement value clause. we have reviewed the reinstatement documents / invoices submitted by the insured. subject to the reinstatement condition being complied with, the loss is assessed on reinstatement basis. any previous indemnity / interim payment should be deducted from the final rvc balance. suggested wording — rvc invoice error but insurer instructed payment short wording the insured had submitted invoices in support of their rvc compliance. upon review, we noted discrepancy / calculation error in the invoice submitted. notwithstanding the above, our adjustment remains unchanged. we understand insurers have advised to proceed with settlement on rvc basis. we therefore record the rvc payment as per insurers’ instruction. more formal wording the insured had subsequently submitted invoices / supporting documents in support of the reinstatement value condition. upon review of the documents submitted, we noted certain discrepancy / calculation error in the invoice / computation. the said discrepancy does not alter our previous adjustment, and our recommended adjusted amount remains unchanged. we understand from insurers / ce that the claim is to be settled on rvc basis. accordingly, the balance rvc amount is released / recommended for payment in accordance with insurers’ instruction. protective wording for record purposes, we wish to highlight that the invoice submitted by the insured contains discrepancy / calculation error. our adjustment has not been revised as the discrepancy does not affect the adjusted amount previously recommended. the settlement on rvc basis is made pursuant to insurers’ instruction. our recommendation is therefore subject to insurers’ confirmation and approval. if the invoice amount is wrong but rvc still payable although the invoice submitted appears to contain calculation error, the reinstatement / replacement works have been substantially supported. our adjusted rvc amount remains as previously assessed. we understand insurers have no objection for the claim to be settled on rvc basis. the balance payable is therefore calculated based on our adjusted rvc figure, less previous payment / interim payment, if any. if insurer directly instructs payment we note insurers’ instruction to proceed with payment on rvc basis. in view of the above instruction, we maintain our adjustment and recommend release of the balance amount accordingly. the invoice discrepancy is noted, but no revision is made to our adjustment unless otherwise instructed by insurers. rvc compliance — invoice submitted / error noted / insurer advised payment circumstances insured submitted invoice / supporting documents to comply with rvc requirement. the documents were submitted after earlier settlement / adjustment. the invoice was reviewed for reinstatement / replacement compliance. later review showed a discrepancy / calculation error in the invoice / computation. our review the invoice discrepancy was noted. the discrepancy does not change our adjusted amount. our adjustment remains as previously recommended. the payable amount should be based on our adjusted rvc figure, not the erroneous invoice computation. insurer’s instruction insurer / ce advised to proceed with settlement on rvc basis. payment is therefore recorded as being made pursuant to insurer’s instruction. any previous interim / indemnity payment should be deducted from the rvc balance. suggested wording the insured had submitted invoices in support of rvc compliance. upon review, we noted discrepancy / calculation error in the invoice submitted. the discrepancy does not alter our previous adjustment and our recommended adjusted amount remains unchanged. we understand insurers have advised to proceed with settlement on rvc basis. accordingly, the balance rvc amount is recommended for payment in accordance with insurers’ instruction. key point if invoice contains error, do not blindly follow the invoice total. maintain the adjusted figure if our adjustment is correct. record that rvc payment follows insurer’s instruction. deduct any previous payment from the final rvc balance.

documents.title: Temporary Work Clause
documents.excerpt: Covers temporary works if included in policy / contract value.

headings.text: Crucial Application
What to check
Suggested wording

documents.searchText: temporary work clause temporary work clause.md policy & clauses crucial application what to check suggested wording endorsement clauses car contractor all risk meaning covers temporary works if included in policy / contract value. temporary works can include formwork, scaffolding, falsework, shoring, temporary supports, temporary decking and temporary structures. not all temporary works are automatically covered. crucial application apply when formwork / scaffolding / temporary support is damaged or missing. example: missing formwork. formwork collapsed during concreting. temporary support failed. scaffolding damaged by storm. what to check is temporary works included in insured property? is formwork / scaffolding part of contract value? is it owned / rented by contractor? is it reusable contractor’s equipment? was it damaged or merely missing? is theft covered? is there supporting inventory / invoices? is it excluded as contractor’s plant / equipment? suggested wording the claim concerns temporary works / formwork at the project site. we have requested documents to confirm ownership, value, location and whether the item forms part of the insured contract works or contractor’s temporary equipment. policy liability is subject to the car wording, insured property description and any exclusion relating to temporary works / contractor’s plant and equipment.

documents.title: Testing and Commissioning Clause
documents.excerpt: Covers testing / commissioning phase if endorsed.

headings.text: Meaning
Crucial Application
What to check
Suggested wording

documents.searchText: testing and commissioning clause testing and commissioning clause.md policy & clauses meaning crucial application what to check suggested wording endorsement clauses car contractor all risk testing and commissioning clause meaning covers testing / commissioning phase if endorsed. important for m&e works, plant, equipment, systems, elevators, generators, pumps, switchboards and machinery. if testing and commissioning is not covered, damage during testing may be doubtful. crucial application apply when loss occurs during: trial run energisation pressure testing load testing wet testing system commissioning functional test handover test what to check is testing and commissioning covered? is testing period stated? is testing within policy period? is hot testing / loaded testing covered? what equipment was being tested? was method statement followed? was manufacturer / specialist present? was failure due to defect / overload / wrong operation? suggested wording the loss occurred during testing / commissioning works. we recommend insurers / operators confirm whether testing and commissioning is covered under the car policy and whether the loss occurred within the allowed testing period. liability remains subject to policy wording, cause of loss and applicable exclusions.

documents.title: Trace And Access Clause
documents.excerpt: Trace and Access Clause covers reasonable cost to locate the source of leakage / damage.

headings.text: Simple Meaning
Key Point
Application Sample
Facts
Suggested wording

documents.searchText: trace and access clause trace and access clause.md policy & clauses simple meaning key point application sample facts suggested wording endorsement clauses 3. trace and access clause simple meaning trace and access clause covers reasonable cost to locate the source of leakage / damage. it may also cover making good damage caused by tracing / accessing the source. it does not automatically cover the repair of the defective pipe / leaking part unless wording allows. key point separate: cost to locate source cost to access source cost to make good access damage cost to repair defective pipe / part application sample facts water leakage occurred. wall / ceiling had to be opened to locate source. source of leakage was concealed pipe. suggested wording the policy is noted to include trace and access clause. we have considered the reasonable cost incurred to locate and access the source of leakage. the subsequent making good of damage caused during tracing / access is also considered, subject to policy terms and limits.

documents.title: Unidentified Clause or Unclear Clause
documents.excerpt: Sometimes policy / report refers to a clause but the exact wording is unclear or not provided.

headings.text: Key Point
Application Sample
Facts
Suggested wording

documents.searchText: unidentified clause or unclear clause unidentified clause or unclear clause.md policy & clauses key point application sample facts suggested wording endorsement clauses simple meaning sometimes policy / report refers to a clause but the exact wording is unclear or not provided. do not assume effect of clause. request copy of endorsement / policy wording. key point clause title alone is insufficient. application sample facts policy schedule mentions a clause. full wording not available. liability depends on clause interpretation. suggested wording we noted that the policy schedule refers to [clause name]. however, the full wording of the clause has not been sighted. we recommend insurers / operators provide / confirm the applicable clause wording before we finalise our comments on policy liability.
